name: Milkfish
description: Farmed in large numbers across the region, especially in Davao del Sur and Davao del Norte.
location: Davao del Sur, Davao del Norte

name: Sea Cow
description: A large, herbivorous marine mammal found in seagrass meadows along Davao del Norte and Davao Oriental.

name: Short-bodied Mackerel
description: A popular, fast-swimming species often found in local markets, it is commonly caught in shallow coastal waters.

name: Sting Ray
description: These large rays are found in the open waters of Davao Gulf, often observed by divers in protected marine areas.

name: Green Sea Turtle
description: Nesting areas for this endangered species are found in the islands of Davao Occidental.

name: Goby
description: Commonly found in tide pools and shallow reefs in coastal Davao.

name: Tabios
description: The world’s smallest commercially harvested fish, often found in protected areas around Davao.

name: Sardine
description: Small, oily fish abundant in coastal waters, used in many regional dishes.

name: Freshwater Sardine
description: Endemic to Taal Lake, but also found in specific, isolated freshwater regions of Davao's lakes.

name: Davao Clownfish
description: Symbiotic anemonefish common in reef lagoons.
location: Coral reefs and lagoons of Davao Gulf.

name: Davao Flasher Wrasse
description: Brightly colored wrasse with elaborate mating displays.
location: Outer reef slopes of Davao Oriental.

name: Dugong
description: Vulnerable marine mammal feeding on seagrass.

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: Migratory pelagic species important to fisheries.

name: Goby
description: Small gobies inhabiting tide pools and shallow reefs.

name: Sea Grapes
description: Edible algae contributing to water quality.

name: Reef Octopus
description: Intelligent reef cephalopod capable of camouflage.

name: Fringelip Mullet
description: Algae-grazing coastal fish.

name: Mottled Rabbitfish
description: Herbivorous fish controlling algae on reefs.

name: Giant Trevally
description: Apex reef predator, sought in sport fishing.

name: Milkfish
description: Brackish water fish farmed extensively.

name: Tulingan
description: Small tuna important to local fisheries.

name: Sailfin Snapper
description: Reef predator valued commercially.

name: Mindanao Grouper
description: Groupers inhabiting reefs and rocky areas.

name: Spotted Parrotfish
description: Herbivorous fish grazing algae on reefs.

name: Philippine Seahorse
description: Seahorse species in mangroves and seagrass beds.
location: Mangrove estuaries in Davao del Norte.

name: Davao Wrasse
description: Small wrasses controlling reef invertebrates.
location: Reefs and tide pools in Davao Region.

name: Spanner Crab
description: Deep-water crab harvested for meat.
location: Offshore sandy bottoms of Davao del Sur.

name: Philippine Squid
description: Cephalopod common in coastal fisheries.

name: Camouflage Grouper
description: Large grouper species, mottled coloration.
location: Location: Coral reefs in Davao Oriental.

name: Laced Moray
description: Spotted eel with strong bite; hides in reef holes.

name: Chevron Butterflyfish
description: Coral-feeding fish with black-and-white chevron pattern.

name: Blue Tang
description: Iconic reef surgeonfish with vibrant blue body.

name: Redtoothed Triggerfish
description: Deep-bodied fish with red teeth and dark coloration.

name: Bristle Worm
description: Brightly colored marine worm that scavenges in coral rubble.
location: Under coral and rocks in Davao intertidal zones.

name: Lettuce Coral
description: Leaf-like coral forming layered colonies.

name: Davao Lanternfish
description: Bioluminescent midwater fish active at night.

name: Davao Blenny
description: Algae-eating fish often perched on rocks and corals.
location: Rocky coastal reefs of Davao del Sur.

name: Slender Sole
description: Flatfish camouflaging with sand and reef rubble.

name: Indian Scad
description: Schooling fish supporting local markets; silver body with yellow tinge.
